race_name: Death Ride — Tour of the California Alps
query: Death Ride gran fondo cycling Markleeville

videos.title: Death Ride Documentary Demo Segment
videos.transcript:
A few miles from Markleyville, riders
reach the junction of Highway 89. The
skeleton squad directs them to the left
to begin the first climb. The front and
back side of Monitor Pass makes up
approximately 28 mi of the course.
The support for this portion of the
course is incredible with two water
stops and two full rest
[Music]
stops. Monitor is a tough climb. It
greets the early morning riders with a
10%
grade. Many riders quickly find
themselves with no remaining gears to
shift
into. As riders approach the first rest
stop, the skeleton squad delivers the
first pass sticker.
Riders replenish their fluids and choose
from a wide variety of food.
How you doing,
Jared? Smiles, everyone. Come on. It's
not that
bad. As riders crest the summit, they
are treated to majestic views of the
Sierra.
This pass is headquarters for the
CareFlight helicopter. Her crew is ready
at a moment's notice to assist anyone in
need of help. A testimony of the value
that the chamber places on rider
safety. Another thing these riders
appreciate after several miles of
climbing is the downhill.
At the Topaz Junction, the skeleton
squad awards the second pass sticker.
Bike mechanics are on hand to deal with
any necessary repairs. With no time to
waste, it's back on the
road. The final water stop on the east
side of Monitor Pass puts death riders
in right of passage
territory. If these guys don't get
riders pumped up, nothing
will.
Their valet style water service is like
no other.
These young men run down the hill to
meet thirsty riders, take their water
bottles, run back to the water station
to fill them, and runs them back to the
owner.
All the while they are cheering,
yelling, and encouraging the riders.
You can always hear the right of passage
group well before you see them.
Looking good out
there.
Their location is well situated because
shortly after passing by them, there's
about 5 miles of steep climbing ahead.
Let's go.
passion. Yes, sir. Let's go. Oh man,
this ride is great. I'm a little tired,
though. I was nervous last night, but it
turned out to be all right. I see why
why they call it the death ride, though.
[Applause]
As riders settle in for another tough
climb, it's back to the summit. Seeing
thousands of riders spread over several
miles is an awesome sight. The energy
from the camaraderie of these riders is
contagious as everyone is in the death
ride spirit.
Quite possibly the most thrilling
downhill on the entire course is the
west side of the path. Speeds can easily
reach 55 mph on the sweeping
turns.
Heat. Heat.

videos.title: The Toughest Bike Ride in Northern California -- Death Ride 2022.  Team Bacon's Journey Impossible
videos.description: Is the course of Death Ride California 2022 tougher than any course in Tour de France?
